calendar.matters:
Petition To Remove Co-Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar May-09-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for and with Turner Washington, Jr.

Mr. Connors stated that he had no contact from the current Co-Trustee, presented oral argument to support his position with regard to bond and stated that he waives Attorney's fees and costs. The Petition to Remove Co-Trustee and Appoint Successor Trustee was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Sandberg.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Third Or Fourth Amended Account
Off Calendar Sep-27-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Turner Washington Jr.

Mr. Connors stated that the matters could probably go off calendar, that both of the Trustees were subject of bankruptcy and that there will be a vacancy that would need to be filled. Both matters were taken off calendar.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Surcharge Successor Co-Trustees And To Redress B Reaches Of Trust
Off Calendar Sep-27-2010 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Third Or Fourth Amended Account
Off Calendar Jul-26-2010 Continued To Sep-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Noell Kubota for Patricia Jefferson and Lawrence Washington (Respondents). Brian Connors for Turner Washington, Jr.

Mr. Connors stated that one of the fiduciaries, Lawrence Washington, had filed bankruptcy, that the other fidiciary Patricia Jefferson was meeting with a bankruptcy Lawyer this Wednesday and requested a 60 day continuance for status of the bankruptcy. In addition, he stated that this would end the surcharge action against them, that it was unlikely that he would seek relief from the bankruptcy stay as it would not be feasible and that there may not be anything left here. The matter was continued to September 27, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

Petition To Surcharge Successor Co-Trustees And To Redress B Reaches Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-26-2010 Continued To Sep-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Third Or Fourth Amended Account
Off Calendar May-10-2010 Continued To Jul-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Surcharge Successor Co-Trustees And To Redress B Reaches Of Trust
Off Calendar May-10-2010 Continued To Jul-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Third Or Fourth Amended Account
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Continued To May-10-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for and with Turner Washington Jr.

Mr. Connors stated that Mr. Kubota asked him to appear for scheduling purposes. The Court clarified that two accountings and the petition for surcharge were before the Court, asked if the Parties had met with a neutral Party to assist with settling the matter and if they would be willing to participate in Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Connors stated that they had not met with anyone, that settling the matter would be unlikely, that he needed to speak to Mr. Kubota about mediation and that he would be willing to attend mediation. The Court stated that it would like a declaration from Mr. Kubota stating that he is interested in participating in Pro Bono Mediation which is to be filed by May 5, 2010. The Court will then sign the order referring the matter to mediation and set mediation for some time in July. The matter was continued to May 10, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Parties participate in mediation and the case does not settle, the Court will set the matter for trial.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Rhonda Aguilar, CSR 956. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition To Surcharge Successor Co-Trustees And To Redress B Reaches Of Trust

Petition To Compel An Accounting And Report
Off Calendar Mar-09-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for and with Turner Washington Jr. (Petitioner). Noell Kubota for and with Patricia Jefferson and for Lawrence Washington (Respondents).

The Court stated that there were a numbers of issues with the accounting. Ms. Kubota stated that she did not know what the current issues were and that the previous issues were addressed in her supplement. Mr. Connors stated that there was no report indicating what the expenses were, questioned the fees, stated that there was no date of death valuation and that there were few documents regarding the payments for money loaned from the trust. The Court stated that the underlying petition to compel the accounting had been complied with and took the matter off calendar. Status on filing of the 3rd or 4th amended account was set for May 4, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy McDaniels.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And Report
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Continued To Oct-27-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brain Connors for Petitioner. Noell Kubota for Patricia Jefferson and Lawrence Washington (Respondents).

Mr. Kubota stated he had retained a paralegal to assist with the accounting, but needed a continuance to give the paralegal additional time to complete it. The matter was continued to October 27, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Hill.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And Report
Off Calendar Aug-11-2008 Continued To Sep-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Noell Kubota for Patricia Jefferson and Lawrence Washington (Respondents). Brian Connors for Turner Washington Jr. (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there were a number of issues on the accounting filed by the Co-Trustees. Mr. Kubota stated that he could amend the format if needed, that he submitted all the receipts he had and that if the Court wanted, the matter could be set for hearing so that Mr. Connors could conduct discovery. Mr. Connors stated that there were many unexplained receipts, that the information should be provided by the Co-Trustees and that the Beneficiaries were entitled an accurate and proper form of an accounting. Mr. Kubota was willing to mediate the accounting issues, but Mr. Connors stated that it was not benefical to mediate. The Court suggested Mr. Kubota use the services of a probate paralegal and provided a list of potential paralegals. The matter was continued to Septmeber 8, 2008 for status on confirmation of selection of a proposed freelance paralegal or alternativley the filing of the second amended acoount and report.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And Report
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Continued To Jul-28-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for Petitioner, Turner Washington, Jr.; Noell Kutota for Respondents, Patricia Jefferson and Lawrence Washington.

Mr. Kutota stated that he had received the Examiner's Notes and will filed the Amended Supplemental Accounting with the court. The Court continued the matter to July 28, 2008 for status. The filing of the Amended Accounting and Report should be done by June 2, 2008. The petition for surcharge of Co-Successor Trustees by Mr. Turner Washington, Jr., to be addressed at the next hearing.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Guitron.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And Report
Off Calendar Apr-07-2008 Continued To May-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for and with Turner Washington, Jr.; Noell Kubota for Trustees Patricia Jefferson adn Lawrence E. Washington.

Mr. Kubota stated that he filed the accounting last Friday. Mr. Connors stated that he has not reviewed the accounting and may file a response. The Court continued the matter to May 19, 2008, any objections may be heard on the same day.
Commissioner: Sue Kaplan; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: R. Hill; Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/C. Herbert.
